summary: Gruber reviews Opera Mini for iPhone and finds it largely disappointing compared to MobileSafari. While Opera Mini offers modest speed advantages on AT&T's 3G network and significant gains on EDGE, MobileSafari is actually faster over Wi-Fi. Beyond speed, Opera Mini suffers from serious deficiencies: security concerns with proxied browsing, poor page zooming, crippled typography, non-native UI controls, and critically flawed scrolling behavior. Gruber notes that Apple's patent on scroll-axis locking may prevent Opera from ever fixing the scrolling problem, and concludes he can only recommend the app to frequent EDGE network users.
key_points: Opera Mini's speed advantage over MobileSafari is marginal on 3G and nonexistent on Wi-Fi, only becoming meaningful on EDGE networks
The proxy-based architecture that enables Opera Mini's speed creates serious security implications for any authenticated browsing
Opera Mini's scrolling feels uncontrolled because it lacks Apple's patented axis-locking heuristic, and Apple's HTC lawsuit suggests Opera may be legally barred from implementing it
Typography is reduced to Helvetica-only rendering, moving in the opposite direction of the web's trend toward richer font choices
The app's UI feels like a web app rather than a native iPhone app, with zooming that works in crude preset chunks rather than smooth arbitrary levels
money_quotes: I would never use Opera Mini to log into a web site whose account or password I value.
The rest of the web is moving toward more font choices, not fewer.
The Opera Mini browser chrome looks like a web app rather than a native iPhone app.
Scrolling an Opera Mini page feels like sliding a sheet of ice on an oil slick.
It's possible that Opera's engineers are perfectly aware of how unpleasant scrolling is in Opera Mini, but they're forbidden from doing anything about it.
I can't see recommending this app to anyone other than those who use the EDGE network frequently.
key_insight: Opera Mini's speed advantage is too narrow and its compromises in security, typography, zooming, and scrolling too severe to justify using it over MobileSafari for anyone with a decent network connection.
